mapping.a_element: q-exponential discount function based on Tsallis' statistics
mapping.b_element: entropic parameter q in nonextensive statistical mechanics

mapping.a_element: parameter q quantifying impulsivity and inconsistency in intertemporal choice
mapping.b_element: parameter q gauging risk aversion/risk-seeking in economic transactions

mapping.a_element: depressive patients exhibiting altered q values (impulsivity/inconsistency)
mapping.b_element: operators with different q values leading to distinct long-term asset redistribution patterns

mapping.a_element: Tsallis' statistics as a framework for modeling decision-making under uncertainty
mapping.b_element: nonextensive statistical mechanics as a framework for modeling economic transactions under risk

brief_justification: Both papers employ Tsallis' nonextensive statistics (via the q-exponential function/entropic parameter q) as the core mathematical machinery to model deviations from classical exponential behavior in decision-making under uncertainty. In A, q captures impulsivity/inconsistency in intertemporal choice, while in B, q captures risk aversion/risk-seeking in economic transactions, but the underlying statistical framework and role of q as a non-classical parameter are identical.
